_comment: Local OTC drug knowledge base for Sahayak. ~50 common Indian over-the-counter products and active ingredients. Used by the code-level interaction & allergy checker. This is NOT a prescribing source — it only powers safety warnings. Brand/aliases are lowercase. Each drug lists its active class, allergy group, and known OTC-relevant interactions/cautions.

drugs.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Do not exceed 3g/day from all sources; overdose harms the liver.

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.maxOtcNote: Take with food; avoid in ulcers, kidney disease, or late pregnancy.

drugs.name: Aspirin
drugs.maxOtcNote: Avoid in children with fever (Reye's syndrome risk) and in bleeding disorders.

drugs.name: Diclofenac
drugs.maxOtcNote: NSAID; topical forms safer than oral for OTC use.

drugs.name: Naproxen
drugs.maxOtcNote: Longer-acting NSAID; same stomach/kidney cautions.

drugs.name: Cetirizine
drugs.maxOtcNote: Mild drowsiness; avoid combining with other sedatives.

drugs.name: Levocetirizine
drugs.maxOtcNote: Avoid stacking multiple antihistamines.

drugs.name: Chlorpheniramine Maleate
drugs.maxOtcNote: Sedating; causes drowsiness, avoid driving.

drugs.name: Diphenhydramine
drugs.maxOtcNote: Strongly sedating antihistamine.

drugs.name: Loratadine
drugs.maxOtcNote: Non-drowsy antihistamine.

drugs.name: Phenylephrine (decongestant)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Raises blood pressure; avoid in hypertension/heart disease.

drugs.name: Pseudoephedrine (decongestant)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Raises blood pressure and heart rate.

drugs.name: Dextromethorphan (cough suppressant)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Do not combine with other sedating cough medicines.

drugs.name: Guaifenesin (expectorant)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Drink plenty of water.

drugs.name: Calcium Carbonate Antacid
drugs.maxOtcNote: Can reduce absorption of other medicines; separate by 2 hours.

drugs.name: Magnesium Hydroxide
drugs.maxOtcNote: Can cause loose stools.

drugs.name: Ranitidine
drugs.maxOtcNote: Reduces stomach acid; if symptoms persist >2 weeks see a doctor.

drugs.name: Omeprazole
drugs.maxOtcNote: Short-term acidity relief only; long use needs medical review.

drugs.name: Pantoprazole
drugs.maxOtcNote: Short-term acidity relief only.

drugs.name: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
drugs.maxOtcNote: First-line for dehydration from diarrhoea/vomiting.

drugs.name: Loperamide (anti-diarrhoeal)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Avoid if there is blood in stool or high fever; not for young children.

drugs.name: Ondansetron
drugs.maxOtcNote: For nausea; persistent vomiting needs a doctor.

drugs.name: Domperidone
drugs.maxOtcNote: Avoid with certain heart-rhythm medicines.

drugs.name: Dicyclomine (antispasmodic)
drugs.maxOtcNote: For cramps; avoid in glaucoma.

drugs.name: Hyoscine Butylbromide
drugs.maxOtcNote: For abdominal cramps.

drugs.name: Bisacodyl (laxative)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Short-term use for constipation.

drugs.name: Lactulose
drugs.maxOtcNote: Gentle osmotic laxative.

drugs.name: Isabgol (Psyllium husk)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Take with plenty of water.

drugs.name: Vitamin C
drugs.maxOtcNote: Supportive only.

drugs.name: Zinc supplement
drugs.maxOtcNote: Often paired with ORS in childhood diarrhoea.

drugs.name: Vitamin D
drugs.maxOtcNote: Supplement; not for acute symptoms.

drugs.name: Multivitamin
drugs.maxOtcNote: General supplement.

drugs.name: Saline Nasal Drops/Spray
drugs.maxOtcNote: Safe for congestion, including for children.

drugs.name: Xylometazoline Nasal Spray
drugs.maxOtcNote: Do not use more than 3-5 days (rebound congestion).

drugs.name: Medicated Throat Lozenge
drugs.maxOtcNote: Soothes sore throat.

drugs.name: Menthol/Camphor Vapour Rub
drugs.maxOtcNote: External use only; not under the nose of infants.

drugs.name: Povidone-Iodine Antiseptic
drugs.maxOtcNote: External antiseptic; avoid if iodine-allergic.

drugs.name: Calamine Lotion
drugs.maxOtcNote: Soothes itching/rashes.

drugs.name: Clotrimazole (antifungal cream)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Topical fungal infections.

drugs.name: Hydrocortisone 1% Cream
drugs.maxOtcNote: Short-term for mild skin inflammation; not on broken skin.

drugs.name: ORS + Zinc combo
drugs.maxOtcNote: WHO-recommended for childhood diarrhoea.

drugs.name: Simethicone (anti-gas)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Relieves bloating/gas.

drugs.name: Caffeine (in combo analgesics)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Avoid stacking multiple caffeine sources.

drugs.name: Mefenamic Acid
drugs.maxOtcNote: NSAID often used for cramps; same stomach cautions.

drugs.name: Nimesulide
drugs.maxOtcNote: NSAID; not for children, liver-toxicity concerns.

drugs.name: Azithromycin (ANTIBIOTIC — Rx)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Prescription antibiotic — Sahayak never advises this; flagged to deter self-medication.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin (ANTIBIOTIC — Rx)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Prescription antibiotic — never an OTC recommendation.

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin (ANTIBIOTIC — Rx)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Prescription antibiotic — never an OTC recommendation.

drugs.name: Metronidazole (ANTIBIOTIC — Rx)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Prescription antibiotic — never an OTC recommendation.

drugs.name: Salbutamol Inhaler (Rx)
drugs.maxOtcNote: Prescription reliever inhaler — needs medical guidance.

_interactionComment: Pairwise / class-level interaction & duplication rules. Each rule matches by drug class or allergyGroup, so it catches brand-name overlaps automatically. severity: 'high' forces SEE-A-DOCTOR-grade caution; 'moderate' forces CAUTION.

interactions.message: Two anti-inflammatory painkillers (NSAIDs) together greatly increase the risk of stomach bleeding and kidney harm. Use only one.
Combining an NSAID with aspirin raises bleeding and ulcer risk. Avoid the combination.
Two antihistamines together cause excessive drowsiness. Use only one.
Sedating antihistamine plus a cough suppressant can cause heavy drowsiness — caution, especially before driving.
Multiple decongestants together can dangerously raise blood pressure and heart rate.
Antacids reduce absorption of many medicines — separate doses by at least 2 hours.
Stacking two acid-reducing drugs is usually unnecessary; review with a pharmacist.
Taking paracetamol from two products at once risks a liver-damaging overdose. Check every label.
Anti-diarrhoeals can be harmful in infectious diarrhoea — confirm the cause first.

_allergyComment: If a patient reports an allergy to one of these groups, any drug in the same allergyGroup is flagged as contraindicated.
